reasoning: Confirmed. The sink at includes/connect-plugins.php lines 307-313 reads $_SERVER['HTTP_REFERER'] directly (not via wp_get_referer()), applies esc_url_raw() which is not a security/origin-validation function, parses the URL query string, and uses query['post'] (after absint()) to select which Polylang language kit to return via pll_get_post(). The attacker can trivially craft a Referer header of the form '?action=elementor&post=<any_id>' to pass the action=elementor gate. absint() prevents injection but does not prevent logical manipulation. The filter fires on ANY REST request (defined('REST_REQUEST') && REST_REQUEST) that causes WordPress to read the elementor_active_kit option, which is broader than just /wp-json/elementor/v1/globals. No authentication check is present on this code path. The concrete impact is that an attacker can cause the wrong-language Elementor Kit (style settings, global colors/fonts) to be served for any REST response that reads this option, and can probe which translated kit post IDs exist by observing response differences. No code execution, no sensitive-data exfiltration, no auth bypass — severity low is appropriate.
